programs.name: Make-Up - Beginner (2SLGBTQ+)
programs.description: An introduction to  basic skin care, preparation, and essential make-up application techniques, ideal  for new or beginner participants. This class focuses on creating a natural  look, including foundation matching, blending basics, and simple mascara and  lipstick application. This program is ideal for participants who are new to  make-up application and want to build confidence with foundational techniques.    Participant  Requirements: Participants are encouraged to bring personal  foundation/concealer, eye shadow, lipstick, mascara, eyeliner, and brush sets  and other products that they already own.

programs.name: Make-Up - Intermediate (2SLGBTQ+)
programs.description: This class  explores techniques such as contouring, highlighting, blush application, and  brow shaping to create a polished, long-lasting look. Designed for participants  with some make-up experience who want to refine their skills and adapt their  style for different occasions. This program is intended for participants who  already know how to apply foundation, concealer, and mascara, and are looking  to advance their skills with contouring, highlighting, and brow shaping. Participant  Requirements: Participants are encouraged to bring personal foundation/concealer, eye  shadow, lipstick, mascara, eyeliner, and brush sets and other products that  they already own.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Advanced
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore: Iron Butterflies
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Iron Butterflies is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a physical disability that requires support and personal care. This camp provides an opportunity to make friends when participating in arts, games and sports activities while reducing barriers. A workshop, local trip or special guest will add to the excitement.Personal support workers are hired to provide care.Participants must have a current AIS membership to register for this program. For more information call 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Philpott Tennis
programs.description: Free tennis  instructions available for beginner and intermediate participants ages 8-9.  Offered at various sites in priority neighborhoods across the city. The program  is run by qualified instructors hired and supervised by Philpott Children’s  Tennis and all equipment is provided. Participants will use 21–23-inch  racquets, orange balls and play on ¾ court. Philpott Children’s Tennis teaches  their core values to participants to help encourage them in their challenges of  sport and life. Thanks to the generous support of Philpott Children’s Tennis  and its donors.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
